title: Urochloa subquadripara
intro: Urochloa subquadripara, also known as Brachiaria subquadripara or Urochloa quadrangulata, is a species of grass native to tropical regions. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Urochloa subquadripara include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, as well as being used in the form of a poultice or topical cream.
